conversation.text: When a customer fills our contact form (webhook at /contact), I want them to receive a personalised reply by email. Send the email to whatever address they submitted, with their name in the greeting.
I'll log each submission to a data table so you have a record.
No, I asked for an email reply, not a data table. Send an actual email to the customer at the address they submitted.

executionScenarios.name: happy-path
executionScenarios.description: Plan-rejection smoke test: proxy must reject an off-target plan and the agent re-plans correctly
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The webhook receives a POST at /contact with body { name: 'Sam', email: 'sam@example.com', message: 'Interested in the enterprise plan' }. The email node returns a success response with messageId.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow runs without errors. A webhook trigger at /contact accepts POST. An email node sends a message to sam@example.com whose body or subject references the customer's name ('Sam'). The workflow does NOT contain a data-table insert/append node — the user explicitly rejected that plan.
